message: Import "m" with LazyMotion instead of "motion" — saves ~30kb in bundle size

message: setMessageInput(messageInput + ...) — use functional update to avoid stale closures
help: Use the callback form: `setState(prev => prev + 1)` to always read the latest value

message: Multiple sequential element.style assignments — batch with cssText or classList for fewer reflows
help: Batch DOM/CSS reads and writes — interleaving them inside a loop causes layout thrashing. Read first, then write

help: Use `cubic-bezier(0.16, 1, 0.3, 1)` (ease-out-expo) for natural deceleration — objects in the real world don't bounce

message: "startHighlight" is read only inside `addEventListener` — wrap it with useEffectEvent and remove it from the dep array so the effect doesn't re-synchronize on every parent render
help: Wrap the callback with `useEffectEvent(callback)` (React 19+) and call the resulting binding from inside the sub-handler. The Effect Event captures the latest props/state without being a reactive dep, so the effect doesn't re-subscribe on every parent render. See https://react.dev/reference/react/useEffectEvent

message: useEffect reacts to "units" which is set by another useEffect — chains of effects add an extra render per link and become rigid as code evolves. Compute what you can during render and write all related state inside the event handler that originally fires the chain
help: Compute as much as possible during render (e.g. `const isGameOver = round > 5`) and write all related state inside the event handler that originally fires the chain. Each effect link adds an extra render and makes the code rigid as requirements evolve

message: "performUndo" is read only inside `addEventListener` — wrap it with useEffectEvent and remove it from the dep array so the effect doesn't re-synchronize on every parent render
help: Wrap the callback with `useEffectEvent(callback)` (React 19+) and call the resulting binding from inside the sub-handler. The Effect Event captures the latest props/state without being a reactive dep, so the effect doesn't re-subscribe on every parent render. See https://react.dev/reference/react/useEffectEvent

message: "wheel" listener without { passive: true } — blocks scrolling performance. Only add { passive: true } if the handler does NOT call event.preventDefault() (passive listeners silently ignore preventDefault())
help: Add `{ passive: true }` as the third argument: `addEventListener('scroll', handler, { passive: true })`. Only do this if the handler does NOT call `event.preventDefault()` — passive listeners silently ignore `preventDefault()`, which breaks features like pull-to-refresh suppression, custom gestures, and nested-scroll containment.

message: Import "m" with LazyMotion instead of "motion" — saves ~30kb in bundle size
help: Use `import { LazyMotion, m } from "framer-motion"` with `domAnimation` features — saves ~30kb

message: Component "UnitListingWizard" has 6 useState calls — consider useReducer for related state
help: Group related state: `const [state, dispatch] = useReducer(reducer, { field1, field2, ... })`

message: useState "mounted" is updated but never read in the component's return — use useRef so updates don't trigger re-renders
help: Replace useState with useRef when the value is only mutated and never read in render — `ref.current = ...` updates without re-rendering the component

message: Component "UnitListingWizard" is 384 lines — consider breaking it into smaller focused components
help: Extract logical sections into focused components: `<UserHeader />`, `<UserActions />`, etc.

message: Date.now() reachable from JSX renders differently on server vs client — wrap in useEffect+useState (client-only) or add suppressHydrationWarning to the parent if intentional
help: Wrap dynamic time/random values in useEffect+useState (client-only) or add suppressHydrationWarning to the parent if intentional

message: .filter().map() iterates the array twice — combine into a single loop with .reduce() or for...of
help: Combine `.map().filter()` (or similar chains) into a single pass with `.reduce()` or a `for...of` loop to avoid iterating the array twice

message: .map().filter() iterates the array twice — combine into a single loop with .reduce() or for...of
help: Combine `.map().filter()` (or similar chains) into a single pass with `.reduce()` or a `for...of` loop to avoid iterating the array twice

message: useState "loading" is updated but never read in the component's return — use useRef so updates don't trigger re-renders
help: Replace useState with useRef when the value is only mutated and never read in render — `ref.current = ...` updates without re-rendering the component

message: fetch() inside useEffect — use a data fetching library (react-query, SWR) or server component
help: Use `useQuery()` from @tanstack/react-query, `useSWR()`, or fetch in a Server Component instead

message: 5 setState calls in a single useEffect — consider using useReducer or deriving state
help: Combine into useReducer: `const [state, dispatch] = useReducer(reducer, initialState)`

message: Three-period ellipsis ("...") in JSX text — use the actual ellipsis character "…" (or `…`)
help: Use the typographic ellipsis "…" (or `…`) instead of three periods — pairs with action-with-followup labels ("Rename…", "Loading…")

message: await inside a for-loop runs the calls sequentially — for independent operations, collect them and use `await Promise.all(items.map(...))` to run them concurrently
help: Collect the items and use `await Promise.all(items.map(...))` to run independent operations concurrently

message: array.includes() in a loop is O(n) per call — convert to a Set for O(1) lookups
help: Use a `Set` or `Map` for repeated membership tests / keyed lookups — `Array.includes`/`find` is O(n) per call

message: Component "TenantTourPage" has 8 useState calls — consider useReducer for related state
help: Group related state: `const [state, dispatch] = useReducer(reducer, { field1, field2, ... })`

message: useState "loading" is updated but never read in the component's return — use useRef so updates don't trigger re-renders
help: Replace useState with useRef when the value is only mutated and never read in render — `ref.current = ...` updates without re-rendering the component

message: useSearchParams() requires a <Suspense> boundary — without one, the entire page bails out to client-side rendering
help: Wrap the component using useSearchParams: `<Suspense fallback={<Skeleton />}><SearchComponent /></Suspense>`

message: Three-period ellipsis ("...") in JSX text — use the actual ellipsis character "…" (or `…`)
help: Use the typographic ellipsis "…" (or `…`) instead of three periods — pairs with action-with-followup labels ("Rename…", "Loading…")

message: .filter().map() iterates the array twice — combine into a single loop with .reduce() or for...of
help: Combine `.map().filter()` (or similar chains) into a single pass with `.reduce()` or a `for...of` loop to avoid iterating the array twice

message: Component "RentApplications" has 39 useState calls — consider useReducer for related state
help: Group related state: `const [state, dispatch] = useReducer(reducer, { field1, field2, ... })`

help: Combine into useReducer: `const [state, dispatch] = useReducer(reducer, initialState)`

message: Destructure for clarity: `const { get } = useSearchParams()` then call `get(...)` directly — easier for React Compiler to memoize and clearer about which methods this component depends on
help: Destructure the method up front: `const { push } = useRouter()` then call `push(...)` directly — clearer dependency graph and easier for React Compiler to memoize

message: fetch() inside useEffect — use a data fetching library (react-query, SWR) or server component
help: Use `useQuery()` from @tanstack/react-query, `useSWR()`, or fetch in a Server Component instead

message: 9 setState calls in a single useEffect — consider using useReducer or deriving state
help: Combine into useReducer: `const [state, dispatch] = useReducer(reducer, initialState)`

message: useEffect schedules `setTimeout(...)` but never returns a cleanup — leaks the registration on every re-run and on unmount. Return a cleanup function that calls clearTimeout(...)
help: Return a cleanup function that releases the subscription / timer: `return () => target.removeEventListener(name, handler)` for listeners, `return () => clearInterval(id)` / `clearTimeout(id)` for timers, or `return unsubscribe` if the subscribe call already returned one

message: useEffect reacts to "fullName" which is set by another useEffect — chains of effects add an extra render per link and become rigid as code evolves. Compute what you can during render and write all related state inside the event handler that originally fires the chain

help: Pass an explicit initial value to `useState` (e.g. `useState("")` instead of `useState()`), add `onChange` (or `readOnly` to opt out) when you supply `value`, and drop `defaultValue` on controlled inputs — React ignores it

message: useSearchParams() requires a <Suspense> boundary — without one, the entire page bails out to client-side rendering
help: Wrap the component using useSearchParams: `<Suspense fallback={<Skeleton />}><SearchComponent /></Suspense>`

message: Destructure for clarity: `const { get } = useSearchParams()` then call `get(...)` directly — easier for React Compiler to memoize and clearer about which methods this component depends on
help: Destructure the method up front: `const { push } = useRouter()` then call `push(...)` directly — clearer dependency graph and easier for React Compiler to memoize

message: fetch() inside useEffect — use a data fetching library (react-query, SWR) or server component

help: Combine into useReducer: `const [state, dispatch] = useReducer(reducer, initialState)`

message: router.push() in useEffect — use redirect() from next/navigation or handle navigation in an event handler
help: Avoid redirects inside useEffect. Use an event handler, middleware, or server-side redirect (App Router: redirect() from next/navigation; Pages Router: getServerSideProps redirect)

message: useContext is superseded by `use()` on React 19+ — `use()` reads context conditionally inside hooks, branches, and loops; switch to `import { use } from 'react'`

message: usePathname() return is only read inside event handlers — defer the read into the handler (e.g. `new URL(window.location.href).searchParams`) so the component doesn't re-render on every URL change
help: Read the URL state inside the handler (e.g. `new URL(window.location.href).searchParams`) so the component doesn't subscribe and re-render on every URL change

message: "reveal" is read only inside `requestAnimationFrame` — wrap it with useEffectEvent and remove it from the dep array so the effect doesn't re-synchronize on every parent render
help: Wrap the callback with `useEffectEvent(callback)` (React 19+) and call the resulting binding from inside the sub-handler. The Effect Event captures the latest props/state without being a reactive dep, so the effect doesn't re-subscribe on every parent render. See https://react.dev/reference/react/useEffectEvent

help: Group related state: `const [state, dispatch] = useReducer(reducer, { field1, field2, ... })`

message: useSearchParams() requires a <Suspense> boundary — without one, the entire page bails out to client-side rendering

help: Combine into useReducer: `const [state, dispatch] = useReducer(reducer, initialState)`

message: Destructure for clarity: `const { get } = useSearchParams()` then call `get(...)` directly — easier for React Compiler to memoize and clearer about which methods this component depends on
help: Destructure the method up front: `const { push } = useRouter()` then call `push(...)` directly — clearer dependency graph and easier for React Compiler to memoize

message: z-index: 9999 is arbitrarily high — use a deliberate z-index scale (1–50). Extreme values signal a stacking context problem, not a fix
help: Define a z-index scale in your design tokens (e.g. dropdown: 10, modal: 20, toast: 30). Create a new stacking context with `isolation: isolate` instead of escalating values

message: 9 inline style properties — extract to a CSS class, CSS module, or styled component for maintainability and reuse

message: A form label must have accessible text.
help: Ensure the label either has text inside it or is accessibly labelled using an attribute such as `aria-label`, or `aria-labelledby`. You can mark more attributes as accessible labels by configuring the `labelAttributes` option.

message: useEffect reacts to "notes" which is set by another useEffect — chains of effects add an extra render per link and become rigid as code evolves. Compute what you can during render and write all related state inside the event handler that originally fires the chain
help: Compute as much as possible during render (e.g. `const isGameOver = round > 5`) and write all related state inside the event handler that originally fires the chain. Each effect link adds an extra render and makes the code rigid as requirements evolve

message: "fetchCounts" is read only inside `on` — wrap it with useEffectEvent and remove it from the dep array so the effect doesn't re-synchronize on every parent render
help: Wrap the callback with `useEffectEvent(callback)` (React 19+) and call the resulting binding from inside the sub-handler. The Effect Event captures the latest props/state without being a reactive dep, so the effect doesn't re-subscribe on every parent render. See https://react.dev/reference/react/useEffectEvent

message: useEffect reacts to "mounted" which is set by another useEffect — chains of effects add an extra render per link and become rigid as code evolves. Compute what you can during render and write all related state inside the event handler that originally fires the chain
help: Compute as much as possible during render (e.g. `const isGameOver = round > 5`) and write all related state inside the event handler that originally fires the chain. Each effect link adds an extra render and makes the code rigid as requirements evolve

message: Vague button label "Done" — name the action ("Save changes", "Send invite", "Delete account") so screen readers and hesitant users know what happens
help: Name the action: "Save changes" instead of "Continue", "Send invite" instead of "Submit", "Delete account" instead of "OK". The label IS the button's accessible name

message: Sequential `await` without a data dependency on the previous result — wrap the independent calls in `Promise.all([...])` so they race instead of waterfalling
help: Wrap independent awaits in `Promise.all([...])` so they race instead of waterfalling — second call doesn't depend on the first

message: A form label must be associated with a control.
help: Either give the label a `htmlFor` attribute with the id of the associated control, or wrap the label around the control.

message: Component "UnitTransferRequest" has 8 useState calls — consider useReducer for related state
help: Group related state: `const [state, dispatch] = useReducer(reducer, { field1, field2, ... })`

help: Use `useSyncExternalStore(subscribe, getSnapshot, getServerSnapshot)` or add `suppressHydrationWarning` to the element

help: Hoist `new Intl.NumberFormat(...)` to module scope or wrap in `useMemo` — Intl constructors allocate dozens of objects per locale lookup
